content.content:
---
title: "Protocol 2026-W01: Base Building"
date: 2026-02-24
week_number: W01
tags: ["protocol"]
phase: "Base Building"
focus: "Swim Technique, Bike Form, Run Maintenance, Strength Preservation"
target_compliance: 85%
---

## Weekly Schedule

| Day | Type | Workout |
|-----|------|---------|
| Monday | Swim | 800m swim with technique drills: 4x100m freestyle (focus on breathing and form), 4x100m kickboard, 4x100m pull buoy |
| Tuesday | Strength | Workout A: Close grip bench press 3x8 @ 115 lbs, Cable seated row 3x11 @ 120 lbs, Barbell shoulder press 3x7 @ 85 lbs, Assisted pull up 3x2 @ 50 lbs, Dumbbell lying tricep extension 3x9 @ 20 lbs, Dumbbell incline curl 3x8 @ 20 lbs, Walking lunges 3x8 @ 20 lbs, Lying leg curl 3x10 @ 125 lbs |
| Wednesday | Bike | 60min indoor trainer: 20min warm-up, 30min at 85-95rpm, 10min cool-down |
| Thursday | Run | 4mi easy run (HR <150) |
| Friday | Strength | Workout B: Dumbbell bench press 3x10 @ 65 lbs, Dumbbell rows 3x11 @ 75 lbs, Cable tricep push downs 3x10 @ 140 lbs, Cable bicep curl 3x9 @ 100 lbs, Dumbbell overhead lateral raise 3x10 @ 12 lbs, Machine seated leg press 3x10 @ 330 lbs, Lying hamstring curls 3x9 @ 130 lbs |
| Saturday | Brick | 30min bike + 20min run (low intensity, focus on transitions) |
| Sunday | Rest | Active recovery: 30min walk, stretching, foam rolling |

## Training Goals
- Establish a consistent swim routine focusing on technique
- Build a solid base on the bike with proper form and cadence
- Maintain running volume while keeping intensity low
- Preserve full body strength with 2x/week full body workouts

## Weekly Targets

**Cardio Volume:**
- Swim: 800m
- Bike: 60 minutes
- Run: 4 miles

**Strength:**
- Increase reps on cable bicep curl and cable tricep push downs by 1 rep per set
- Maintain current weights on other lifts, focus on form and progressive overload

## AI Rationale

This protocol is designed to help Ryan transition smoothly from marathon running to triathlon training while preserving his strength. Given that he is a beginner in swimming and cycling, the initial focus is on technique and form to prevent injuries and build a strong foundation. The swim session is kept short and focused on drills to ensure he masters the basics. The bike session is also moderate, emphasizing form and cadence to build endurance gradually. Running is maintained at a low intensity to leverage his existing base without overloading his body.

Strength training is crucial to prevent muscle loss and support overall fitness. By alternating between two full-body workouts, Ryan can maintain balanced development and ensure progressive overload. The inclusion of a brick workout on Saturday helps him practice transitions, which is essential for triathlon performance.

The rest day on Sunday ensures adequate recovery, which is critical for sustained high performance. This balanced approach aligns with Ryan's goal of achieving top 0.01% performance across multiple domains while managing his startup and content creation responsibilities.

content.content:
---
title: "Protocol 2026-W07: Volume Building"
date: 2026-02-16
week_number: W07
tags: ["protocol"]
phase: "Volume Building"
focus: "Hypertrophy + Triathlon Base"
target_compliance: 85%
---

## Training Philosophy
- All sets to **1 rep in reserve (1 RIR)** — stop when you could do one more
- **Supersets** where marked to keep sessions tight
- Progression rule: if first set hits 12+ reps at 1 RIR, add 5 lbs next week
- Log reps as you go — no fixed rep targets, just weight and effort

## Weekly Schedule

| Day | Type | Workout |
|-----|------|---------|
| Monday | Swim | 400-800m freestyle (scale to energy) |
| Tuesday | Strength A | See below |
| Wednesday | Bike | 45 min indoor trainer (85-95 RPM, HR <150) |
| Thursday | Run | 4 miles easy (HR <150) |
| Friday | Strength B | See below |
| Saturday | Brick | 30 min bike + 20 min run |
| Sunday | Active Recovery | 30 min light swim or walk + stretching |

## Strength A (Tuesday)

Based on Feb 10 actuals with progression applied.

| Exercise | Weight | Notes |
|----------|--------|-------|
| Incline bench press | **120 lbs** | Up from 115. Hit 17/9/7 last week |
| Cable seated row | **145 lbs** | Up from 140. Dominated at 140 (20/11/11) |
| Overhead press | **85 lbs** | Hold weight. Aim for more consistency across sets (was 10/7/3) |
| Assisted chin-ups | **70 lbs assist** | Down from 75 assist. Hit 4/3/2 last week |
| Dumbbell lying tricep extension | 20 lbs | No recent data — hold and log |
| Dumbbell incline curl | 20 lbs | No recent data — hold and log |
| Walking lunges | **25 lbs** | Up from 20. Hit 12/10/7 last week |
| Lying hamstring curls | **130 lbs** | Up from 125. Nearly hit 10/10/10 |

**Supersets (suggested):**
- A1: Incline bench / A2: Cable seated row
- B1: Overhead press / B2: Assisted chin-ups
- C1: Tricep extension / C2: Incline curl
- D1: Walking lunges / D2: Hamstring curls

## Strength B (Friday)

Based on Feb 6 actuals (partial session) + Feb 15 (completed, no logged details). Progression conservative since data is limited.

| Exercise | Weight | Notes |
|----------|--------|-------|
| Dumbbell bench press | **65 lbs** | Up from 60. Hit 13/11/8 on Feb 6 |
| Dumbbell rows | **75 lbs** | Up from 70. Hit 13/12/7 on Feb 6 |
| Cable tricep push downs | 140 lbs | Hold — no recent rep data |
| Cable bicep curl | 100 lbs | Hold — no recent rep data |
| Dumbbell overhead lateral raise | **12 lbs** | Up from 10. Hit 14/10/8 on Feb 6 |
| Machine seated leg press | **330 lbs** | Up from 320. No recent data but conservative bump |
| Lying hamstring curls | **130 lbs** | Match Strength A progression |

**Supersets (suggested):**
- A1: DB bench press / A2: DB rows
- B1: Tricep push downs / B2: Bicep curls
- C1: Lateral raise / C2: (rest or core work)
- D1: Leg press / D2: Hamstring curls

## Volume Progression Plan

| Week | Sets per exercise | Goal |
|------|-------------------|------|
| W07 (this week) | 3 sets | Establish baseline at new weights |
| W08 | 3 sets | Push reps higher at same weight |
| W09 | 4 sets | Add volume |
| W10 | 4 sets | Push weight if reps are high |
| W11 | Deload | Drop to 2 sets, reduce weight 10% |

## Cardio Targets
- Swim: 400-800m (based on energy)
- Bike: 45 minutes
- Run: 4 miles
- Brick: 30+20 min

## Key Rules
- Log every set (weight x reps) — this is how we progress
- Wear the Garmin for every cardio session
- If gym is packed (Fridays), go earlier or swap to Saturday
